lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, Chandler, Monica, Phoebe, and Joey are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait a minute, wait. You're telling me this actress person is the only woman you ever wanted who didn't want you back?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Oh my God! (to Chandler) Is this what it's like to be you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow, you're really crazy about her, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you have no idea. And-and when we're on stage I get to-to kiss her and-and touch her, but then she goes home with the director, and it's like somebody's ripping out my heart!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's so great to see you feeling like this!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica, uh Dad called this morning and ah, Aunt Silvia passed away.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!! Yes! Yeah, yeah, yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We were all pretty shaken up about it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, am I missing something though? 'Cause I thought death was something that's supposed to be sad, in a way.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well ah, Aunt Silvia was, well not a nice person.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, she was a cruel, cranky, old bitch! (Ross gives her a look) (to Ross) And I'm sorry she died. Did Dad say I get the dollhouse?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You get the dollhouse.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I get the dollhouse!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, a house for dolls, that is so cool! When I was kid, I had a barrel.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, Pheebs, you had a barrel for a dollhouse?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, just a barrel.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know what, you can play with my dollhouse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?! Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Any time you want. Y'know, when I was younger, all I wanted to do was to play with this dollhouse, but no!! It was to be looked at, but never played with.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My Grandmother used to say that exact same thing to me.
Opening Credits
Rachel's office, Chandler and her are coming back from lunch.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey, Sophie!

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Hey, Rach!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thanks for lunch, Chandler. Y'know, you didn't have to walk me all the way back up here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, that's-that's okay, no problem. (He starts to look around her office.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey um, honey, you do realise that we don't keep the women's lingerie here in the office?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, I realise that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Summer catalogue! (hands him the catalogue)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's the stuff! (quickly grabs it)
[Rachel's boss, Joanna, enters]

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Rachel, I need the Versachi invoice. (to Chandler) Hello! You don't work for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (introduces them) Joanna, this is my friend Chandler Bing (to Chandler) Joanna.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Bing! That's a great name.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thanks, it's ah, Gaelic, for 'Thy turkey's done.' So ah, I'm gonna go, nice, nice meeting you.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (to Rachel) So ah, what's wrong with him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, nothing, he's just goofy like that, I actually, hardly notice it anymore.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh no, no-no-no, is he ah, married, or involved with anyone?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!! No! He's not married, or involved, with anyone!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh, Rachel, (pause) actually, y'know what, forget it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'll ask him for you, if you want me too?

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Would you? Or, is it just to sad and desperate, and y'know something that Sophie would do?

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Uh, uh, uh, I am here.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: I know that.
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is showing off her new dollhouse. It's a huge dollhouse, that takes up the entire living room table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look at it! Ohhh! Wallpaper's a little faded, that's okay. Carpet's a little loose. Hardwood floors!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Ooh! Oh Monica! It's so beautiful.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So, I'm here, ready to play.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I brought a bunch of stuff for the house, so check it out. Ha-ha. (She removes this large ceramic dog that comes up to the second floor and places it next to the house.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's a dog, every house should have a dog.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not one that can pee on the roof.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, maybe it's so big because the house was built on radioactive waste.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (holding a tissue) And is this in case the house sneezes?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, that's the ghost for the attic.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't want a ghost.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, nobody wants a ghost. But you've got one, because the house is sitting on an ancient Indian burial ground.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait a minute, the house was built on radioactive waste, and an ancient Indian burial ground? That would never happen.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, obviously you don't know much about the U.S. government.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I need to talk to you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure, what's up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, sorry. I meant Chandler.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I know. Well if something comes up... (walks away)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I'm glad you guys are past that little awkward phase.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, my boss, Joanna, when you left, she started asking questions about you...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-ho, liked what she saw, huh? Dug my action, did she? Checkin' out the Chan-Chan man!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (looks at him) That was (pause) surreal. Okay, what do think? Are you interested at all?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, she seemed cool, attractive. I'll do it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh thank you, Chandler, this is so great, she's gonna love me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (holding a dinosaur) Okay, dinosaur attack!! Quick, everybody into the house!! Ahh-ahh! (the dinosaur starts attacking the house. She starts to bark like a dog.) Roof! Rrroof-roof-roof!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, Phoebe, y'know what? That-that's it, that's it, all right? No dinosaurs, no ghosts, no giant dogs, okay? They're not the right size, they're not Victorian, and they just don't go.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, (starts to pack up her stuff) fine. Come dinosaur, we're not welcome in the house of no imagination.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Pheebs, while we're hovering around the subject. I just have to say dinosaurs, they-they don't go, rrroof!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The little ones do.
The Theatre, Joey and Kate are getting ready to rehearse the play.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Kate!

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Morning.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen, I ah, went to that restaurant that you were talking about last week...

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Hey, lovely! Come, talk to me a minute! (she goes over to him)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to himself) And I ate the food, I had the fish, it was good, yeah. It was good, yeah...

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi, oh, I'm Lauren, Kate's understudy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, hey! Joey Tribbiani.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: I know! I-I'm a big fan of yours.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (looks at her, shocked) What?!

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: I used to umm, schedule my classes so I could watch Dr. Drake Remoray on Days of Our Lives.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Get out of here, really?!

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Absolutely!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Oh but then, they went and dropped you down that elevator shaft.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They gave me the shaft all right.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (laughing) Oh, you're so funny. Listen, umm, what are you doing after rehearsals? Do you want to get a drink, or something?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well Ahh, (he sees Kate and the director kissing) yeah! Yeah, sure, a drink sounds great.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Cool! I-I'll see you then.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: All right, it's time to act, my talking props. (Both Joey and Kate just look at each other.)
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is entering with her own dollhouse, that she made herself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look everybody, look at my new dollhouse!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Look, look! (She lifts up the roof, and the front panel falls revealing the interior.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, what's this?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, it's the slide instead of stairs. Watch this. (She slides a doll down the slide)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's very interesting, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The Licorice Room, you can eat all the furniture. And, when guests come over, they can stay on the tootsie roll-away bed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is the coolest house ever!!
Monica is looking on with a hurt expression on her face.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, does anybody want to join me in the aroma room? (lights some incense)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I would!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, guys, guys, did you see my new, china cabinet?!

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Watch, watch. (She turns a strand of Christmas lights strung around the house.)

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Ooohhhh!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And, and! (She turns on a bubble maker.)

lines.speaker: Ross and Rachel
lines.text: Ahhhh!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey, my Father's house does that!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Chandler) O-o-o-okay, how did it go? Tell me everything.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, the movie was great, dinner was great, and there's nothing like a cool, crisp New York evening.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Of course, I didn't get to enjoy any of that, because Joanna's such a big, dull dud!
Rachel's office, Joanna's telling Rachel, her side of the story.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Chandler is fantastic!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh God, we just clicked! Y'know how people just click? Like he came by to pick me up, and I opened the door, and it was just like, click! Did he tell you?

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh, and he's got such a good heart! Doesn't he have a good heart?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I know...

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Oh, I know and he's soo sweet! Listen, he said he was going to call, so put him straight through.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Isn't this great?!

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Don't spoil it.
The Theatre, Joey and Kate are rehearsing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on baby, don't go. Please? What do you say?
[A phone rings.]

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: (answering the phone) Hello. Oh! It's you. Just ah, just one-one sec. (to Joey and Kate) I am going to take this call. When I continue, I hope that there will appear on stage this magical thing that in the theatre we call, committing to the moment! (He goes to take the call.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Kate) That guy's like a cartoon. What do you see in him anyway?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: He happens to be brilliant. Which is more than I can say for that sweater you're dating.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I'm not interested in her sweater! It's what's underneath her sweater that counts. And besides, since ah, since when do you care who I'm going out with?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I don't care. Why, do you want me to care?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you want me to want you to care?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Do you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: (returning) Okay, I'm afraid to say this, but let's pick it up where we left off.
[They resume rehearsing.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on baby, don't go. Please? What do you say?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I've got no reason to stay.
Joey grabs her and kisses her.

lines.speaker: The Director
lines.text: Stop!! Stop it! You must stop! You are bad actors! This is a terrible play! I'll see you in the morning. (exits)

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: I can't believe we go on in, in a week.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, it's gonna be all right.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (to Joey) Hey! So since we're getting off early, do you want to go and paint mugs?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: You know! At the place I told you about last night?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, with the mug painting. Yeah. I was so listening to that. But ah, y'know what, I think I kinda need to work on my stuff tonight.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay. (he gives her a peck on the cheek)

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: I'll see you tomorrow. (she kisses him full on the mouth.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: G'night. (exits)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Kate) Ah, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yeah, I guess. Look, what are we gonna do about this scene, huh?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Well umm, maybe if it had more heat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How do you mean?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Well, Adrian's looking for a reason to stay, right? Victor can't just kiss her, he's gotta, gotta really give her a reason, y'know?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Maybe he could slip her the tongue.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Or maybe, maybe he could grab her, and, and, and, and lift her up.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, and then Adrian, she maybe she could wrap her legs around his waist.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: And then she could rip off his shirt and kiss his chest, and, and his stomach!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And then, then he could use his teeth, his teeth to undo her dress, and, and, and bite her!

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: And then right, right when the scene ends, he could take her with this raw, animal....
[cut to Joey's bedroom, Joey and Kate are emerging from under the covers.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Something like that?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Yeah, that's pretty much what I had in mind.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Ross and Monica are eating breakfast as Joey enters, very happily.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross) Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Monica) Hey.
He walks up behind Monica and gives her a big hug and a kiss on the neck.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.
He walks over behind Ross, thinks about it for a moment, and gives him a big hug.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's a little early to be drinkin'.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, things ah, finally happened with Kate.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohhhhh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're kidding?! That's great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, it was so amazing. After the (pause) love making...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep. I just, I just watched her sleep for like hours, just breathing in and breathing out. And then I knew she was dreaming 'cause, 'cause her eyes keep going like this. (He closes his eyes and moves them around, kinda like he's been processed by the devil, or something.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering with Rachel) I'm telling ya, Joanna's got it all wrong. Okay? All I said was, 'This was fun. Let's do it again sometime. I'll give you a call.'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, gee. I wonder why she thinks you're going to call her?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what you say at the end of a date.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You can't just say, 'Nice to meet you, good night?'

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To her face? Look it's the end of the date, I'm standing there, I know all she's waiting for is for me to say 'I'll call her' and it's just y'know, comes out. I can't help it, it's a compulsion.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come on Rach, when a guy says he's going to call, it doesn't mean he's going to call. Hasn't it ever happened to you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, they always called.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hmm, bite me.
Rachel's office.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: (entering) Did he call?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Sorry.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Why?! Why?! He said he'd call. Why hasn't he called?

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.text: Maybe he's intimated by really smart, strong, successful women.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Sophie, would you please climb out of my butt. Why hasn't he called, Rachel? Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, okay. Umm, well ah, maybe he, maybe he feels awkward because you are my boss.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Awkward? Why should he feel awkward?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: The only person that should feel awkward is you, and you didn't tell him not to call me, did you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. I...

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Because if you feel uncomfortable with your friend dating someone you work for, there are always ways to fix...that.
Central Perk, Chandler is reading a magazine as Rachel approaches.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (grabbing the magazine out of his hands) Call her! Call her now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Multiple, so many paper cuts.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why hasn't he called Rachel? Why? Why? I don't understand. Why? He said he'll call. Why? Why? Chandler I'm telling you she has flipped out, she's gone crazy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (sarcastic) Oh, well give me the phone then.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Come on, this isn't funny. She thinks it's my fault that you haven't called her. You have to call her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, you can't call somebody after this long just to say, 'In case you didn't notice, I don't like you!'

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well then you're going to have to take her out again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nooo!! She's really dull! And she gets this gross mascara goop thing in the corner of her eye!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care! I don't care! You are going to have to take her out again and end it, and end it in way that she knows it's actually ended. And, I don't care how hard it is for you, do not tell her that you will call her again!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right! Fine! But it's just a lunch date, no more than an hour! And from now on I get my own dates, I don't want you setting me up with anybody ever again!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's fine!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's just a lot of big talk, y'know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know.
Monica and Rachel's, Ross is entering.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (from the bathroom) I'm in the shower!

[He closes the door and walks over to get something from the fridge. He starts to smell something and turns around to see Phoebe's dollhouse smoking. He runs over and takes off the roof to reveal that the dollhouse fire.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, fire! There, there's a fire! Fire!!

[He tries to blow it out, and obviously, it doesn't work. He runs over to the sink to get a glass of water to put out the fire, but since Monica is in the shower the water pressure is very low and takes a long time to fill the glass. In desperation he takes the half full glass over and dumps it on the fire, it doesn't work. He then picks up the dollhouse and considers bringing it over to the sink, but decides to take it into the bathroom and use the shower to put it out. He kicks open the door and we hear Monica scream at the top of her lungs.]
The Theatre, Kate is arriving for rehearsal.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So I ah, talked to Lauren, kinda told her how things were with us. Did you ah, did you talk to Marshall?

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: About what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Y'know, about what happened with us.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Nooo. And there's really no reason he should find out, so ah let's not make a big deal about it, okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are you talking about? It was a big deal. I mean, come on you can't tell me last night didn't mean something to you. I-I was there, you're not that good an actress.

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Look umm, I, I was, I was just caught up in the moment. That's all it was. Joey, I'm-I'm sorry you feel bad, but haven't you ever sleep with a women where it meant more to her than it did to you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nooo.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: (entering) Hi, Kate!

lines.speaker: Kate
lines.text: Hi, Lauren.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi, Lauren.

lines.speaker: Lauren
lines.text: Hi, pig!
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Ross are inspecting the damage to the dollhouse.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry I ah, I scared you in there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that's okay. By the way, I was just checking the shower massager.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (runs over to her) I tried to reach you at work. There's....been a fire.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?! Oh my... (sees the remains of the house) Oh my God!! What happened?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we believe it originated here. (He uses a pointer and points to the point of origin.) In the Aroma Room.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. Did everyone get out okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, the giraffe's okay. And so is the pirate.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh. What is this? (She sees a tissue covering something, and moves to remove it.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No Phoebe, don't look! You don't want to see what's under there!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (She pauses to ready herself, and removes the tissue.) Ohh, the-the Foster puppets!
She picks up a charred piece of plastic that once was the Foster puppets, and starts to break down. Monica goes over and comforts her.
Rachel's office, Chandler and Joanna are returning from their lunch date. He is telling her about her mascara problem. Rachel is already there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's not a big deal. It's, just it's right here, (points to his eye) and it's all the time.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Well, thanks again for lunch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (He looks over at Rachel, who nods her head) Yes, this, this was pleasant.
Rachel is slowing trying to leave and let them talk.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: It was, wasn't it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The food there was, was great.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Wasn't it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So take care.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: You too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, this was great. I'll give you a call. We should do it again sometime. (Rachel is disappointed)

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Great! I'm looking forward to it. Rachel, any messages?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sophie's desk.
Chandler starts to leave ashamed of himself, but Rachel stops him in the hallway.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (whispering) Chandler!! Are you gonna call her!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Noo!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, I'm sorry. Okay? I'm weak, and pathetic, and sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, you are going to tell her and you're going to tell her now. (She grabs his nipple and starts to twist it.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ahhhh-I'm not going to call you.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry. I'm-I'm-I'm sorry that I said I was going to when I'm not. Look, this has nothing to do with you, y'know? And this isn't Rachel's fault. It's me. I have serious, serious problems when it comes to women. I have issues with commitment, intimacy, (pause) mascara goop. And I'm really sorry, it's just that this is not, this isn't going to work out.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: Well, this isn't how I was hoping how this would end, but I guess I have to appreciate your honesty.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, o-okay.

lines.speaker: Joanna
lines.text: So...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well this is great! I'll give you a call! We should do it again sometime!
Rachel is shocked, and holds her arms out in disbelief.
Closing Credits
Chandler and Joey's, Joey is talking on the phone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on phone) Well, so anyway Beth, what I'm saying is I should've considered your feelings before I went home with you that night. I've ah, I've recently learned what's it like to be on your side of it, and I'm sorry. So, do you think you can forgive me? (listens) Great. Thanks. Okay, bye. (He sits down and crosses out something, and dials the phone again.) Hello, Jennifer? (listens) Oh hi, Mrs. Loreo, is Jennifer there? (listens) Oh, she's not home huh? (listens) Well ah, actually I kinda need to talk to you too.
End
